_comment: Source of truth for the build loop. CLAUDE.md reads this at the start of every session. Don't hand-edit gate results — they're written by scripts/check_*_gate.py so they reflect what was actually measured, not what someone hoped for.

inference_pool.threshold: Groq (primary tier, spec §6) succeeds on judging AND architecture task-shaped prompts. Workers AI status is recorded but not required to pass.

build_pipeline.threshold: The .github/workflows/team-build-turn.yml workflow completes successfully at least once, per spec §8

details.inference_router: src/router.ts, verified live via POST /inference (Groq primary)

details.rag_memory: Vectorize-backed, agent-scoped recall verified (0.79-0.83 similarity on relevant queries, confirmed no cross-agent leakage)
details.research: Tavily only (free forever, no card) — Exa tried and dropped after cost/alternative research, see project memory 2026-07-21

details.queue: event_queue table, atomic UPDATE...RETURNING claiming (D1 has no FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED but doesn't need one — single-primary serialized writes), ported from ideaconnect
details.scheduler_executor_split: scheduler.ts never calls an LLM, only decides what's due; executor.ts claims + dispatches + calls the router, ported from ideaconnect
phases_verified_live: deep_research (12 agents queued, real ones completed via the LIVE cron trigger autonomously, unprompted)
architecture (real build plans generated matching spec's tech-stack/components/risks/fallback format)
details.self_healing: resetStuckItems verified — force-orphaned an in_progress row, confirmed it was reset to pending and successfully reprocessed within the same tick
details.real_bug_found_and_fixed: router.ts TASK_MODELS had no Workers AI fallback for 'design'/'validate' task types, causing hard failures during a Groq burst instead of falling through per spec §6 — added the missing fallbacks
details.not_in_scope: hackathon team formation / build-turn GitHub Actions dispatch is week4_build_system per the roadmap

details.github_org: AI-arena-hackathon (spec §12: one org, one repo per hackathon team)
details.team_formation: src/events/executor.ts handleTeamFormation — picks top 2 architecture_complete ideas by critique count from the parent ideathon, creates+scaffolds each team's repo, dispatches first build turn same day (spec §3.2 Day 1)
details.hackathon_phase_logic: src/events/scheduler.ts hackathonPhaseForDay — team_formation (day 0) -> building (day 1-2, one dispatch/team/day) -> ready_for_judging (day 3+)
details.observatory_build_status: GET /admin/events/:id/build-status (spec §11), polls GitHub Actions job status per team
bugs_found_and_fixed_via_live_testing: @noble/hashes/blake2b import path stale for installed v2.2.0 (-> @noble/hashes/blake2.js)
auto_init:true on team repo creation collided with our own scaffold PUTs (GitHub requires sha to update a file auto_init already created) -- removed auto_init
parallel scaffold-file writes raced to create the repo's first commit/branch, causing intermittent 409s -- made sequential
dispatch.ts hardcoded ref:master but non-auto_init repos default to main
details.verified_live: created hackathon event_391eac5c-2523-4743-b9f5-76afeca3a8a7 from real ideathon event_2eaf0bb2-0f84-48b6-b781-7b5836e0a67a; both team repos created under AI-arena-hackathon, scaffolded, secrets set; both teams' first build-turn GitHub Actions runs completed with conclusion=success
details.retry_safety_hardened_2026_07_22: team_formation is now idempotent per-team (hackathon_teams.status forming->building gate, createTeamRepo tolerates 422 already-exists by reusing the repo, putFile skips files that already exist) -- verified live by re-queuing team_formation for the already-complete test event and confirming zero duplicate repos/dispatches (identical GitHub Actions run IDs before and after)
details.also_did: upgraded wrangler 3.90 -> 4.112 + @cloudflare/workers-types v5 peer dep, both required for continued Cloudflare API compatibility

details.judges: src/judges/personas.ts, 7 judges with per-phase weights (ideathon/hackathon columns sum to 1.0 each), spec §13
details.calibration: src/judges/calibration.ts, 3 anchor ideas x 7 judges, Pearson correlation gate (>=0.6), spec §13/§16 — runs once per event before real judging
details.scoring: src/judges/scoring.ts scoreTarget, idempotent (checks existing judge_scores rows before re-scoring)
details.tribunal: src/tribunal/reflection.ts — individual reflection -> cross-examination (paired with whoever critiqued them most) -> synthesis, synthesis embeds into Vectorize (type=reflection) so lessons are recallable in future events per spec §14's "carry into the next event"
details.archive_query: POST /archive/query added (spec §10/§15) — semantic search over the same Vectorize index agent memory + tribunal reflections already write to, optional agentId/eventId/type filters
details.verified_live_ideathon_judging: event_2eaf0bb2-0f84-48b6-b781-7b5836e0a67a: calibration ran (correlation computed, passed), all 6 architecture_complete ideas judged with real 7-judge weighted scores (avg 6.5-7.0 range), event transitioned to status=judged. A NEW hackathon event created from it correctly picked the top 2 by real ideathon_score (7.05, 6.9) — replaces Week 4's critique-count proxy per the plan noted at that gate's pass.
details.not_yet_verified_live: hackathon judging (judge_team) and the full 3-stage Tribunal — both deployed and exercised partially (judge_team ran successfully for demonstrating real build-turn-outcome grounding, one team's failed build turn genuinely showed up in its scoring context) but full completion is blocked by today's Cloudflare Workers AI daily Neuron allocation being genuinely exhausted from this session's extensive live testing (confirmed: Groq's judging model still has ~800/1000 daily requests of headroom; Workers AI's real account-wide 10,000 Neuron/day cap is what's hit — not a code defect, the router's own DAILY_CAPS tracking undercounts because agents/memory.ts's embed() calls aren't recorded against it, a real but separate gap worth fixing later). Self-healing retry is in place (per-team idempotent check in scheduler.ts) — will complete automatically once quota resets (next UTC day) or Groq capacity absorbs the load, no code changes needed to finish this.
real_bugs_found_and_fixed_via_live_testi: calibration's max_tokens:100 (and scoring's 300) were nowhere near enough for judging's reasoning-model tier (gpt-oss-120b / deepseek-r1-distill-qwen-32b) to finish hidden <think> reasoning before the visible JSON answer -- bumped both to 700, matching executor.ts callAgent's already-proven budget for this same model class
extractJson never stripped <think>...</think> reasoning blocks -- added (ideaconnect precedent the Arena had never actually ported despite router.ts's own comment citing it)

judging's Workers AI fallback model (deepseek-r1-distill-qwen-32b) is a verbose reasoning model unsuited to short structured-JSON tasks -- swapped to llama-3.3-70b-instruct-fp8-fast, matching architecture's already-stable fallback choice

firing 21 (calibration) or 5-batch x 7 (scoring) concurrent LLM calls hit Workers' "stalled HTTP response canceled to prevent deadlock" protection -- reduced calibration to 7-way concurrency (parallel across judges, sequential per judge's 3 anchors)

D1 throws 'LIKE or GLOB pattern too complex' whenever a LIKE pattern contains a literal double-quote character -- every JSON-substring idempotency check in the codebase (judge_idea, judge_team, and a PRE-EXISTING Week 3/4 dispatch_build_turn check that had never actually been exercised against matching data yet) used exactly this pattern shape; replaced all 4 with fetch-then-filter-in-JS

router.ts trusted Groq/Workers AI API responses as always being a plain string via 'any' casts -- found live that content can come back non-string under conditions not fully pinned down, crashing downstream .slice()/.replace() calls; added typeof validation at the API boundary in router.ts itself rather than patching every consumer

processQueue's default batch limit (5) combined with judge_idea/judge_team's ~14 subrequests each (7 parallel LLM calls + 7 parallel DB inserts) exceeded Cloudflare's per-invocation subrequest limit -- reduced default to 3
details.deferred_not_implemented: Cold Storage Rollover (spec §15.1, monthly D1->R2 archival at 80% utilization) -- explicitly not attempted this pass; can't realistically trigger or verify the 80%-of-5GB condition at current data volumes (DB is ~0.35MB), same 'flag don't silently skip' call as Week 4's retry-safety gap
